title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: How to Make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
easy Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
lowCompetitionKeywords: Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani — a beloved Indian classic

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian dish renowned for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender pieces of chicken. This beloved curry offers a perfect balance of smoky, sweet, and tangy flavors, making it a comforting and incredibly satisfying meal. It is a culinary jewel that truly captures the essence of North Indian cuisine.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Mix well and let it marinate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 2 tablespoons butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Gravy Ingredients
instructions.text: Stir in the pureed tomatoes, soaked cashews, remaining 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, and 1/2 teaspoon garam masala. Add a pinch of salt and sugar. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-12 minutes until the tomatoes soften and the mixture thickens slightly and butter starts to separate.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Gravy
instructions.text: Let the gravy mixture cool slightly, then transfer it to a blender. Blend until very smooth. For an extra silky texture, strain the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing down on solids to extract all liquid.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Return the strained gravy to the pot and bring to a gentle simmer. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces, heavy cream, and the remaining 1 tablespoon butter. Crush the kasoori methi between your palms and add to the gravy. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Adjust salt and sugar if needed. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

tips: For a truly smoky flavor, char the marinated chicken pieces slightly under a broiler or in a tandoor before adding to the gravy.
Always use fresh, ripe tomatoes for the gravy base; for a smoother texture, blanch and peel them before pureeing.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.
article: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
